steps.title: Reduce the angle
steps.content:
Cosine repeats every $2\pi$. Since $2\pi=\frac{12\pi}{6}$, reduce the angle by multiples of $\frac{12\pi}{6}$.
$$\frac{565\pi}{6}=47(2\pi)+\frac{\pi}{6}$$

steps.title: Use the unit circle
steps.content:
The angle is coterminal with $\frac{\pi}{6}$, so the cosine value is the same.
$$\cos(\frac{565\pi}{6})=\cos(\frac{\pi}{6})=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: A calculator can also evaluate the original cosine expression directly. The answer is C.
